items.title: Man found not guilty of murdering Darwin mother a 'serious risk' to community

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was brutally stabbed to death in the yard of her suburban home in 2022. The man charged with her murder never went to trial due to mental impairment but he could be detained for 20 years.

image.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

items.title: Police investigate suspicious death of man found in Alice Springs home

children.content: A 44-year-old man has been found dead in a home in Alice Springs under "suspicious" circumstances, with NT police launching an investigation into the incident.

image.altText: A white street sign in suburbia that reads 'Nicker Cr". Houses and gum trees in background.

items.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'

children.content: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

articles.title: NT Health confirms only one possible diphtheria-related death amid outbreak

children.content: The Northern Territory health department is refuting an earlier report that a second person has died with diphtheria in the NT, amid Australia's largest outbreak of the disease since records began.

image.altText: Alice Springs hospital exterior

items.title: Air and land search continues for missing 31-year-old man in Arnhem Land

children.content: The NT Police Force says it continues to hold concerns for the welfare of the man, who was last seen early on Friday.

image.altText: Man with afro and short beard has neutral expression on face

items.title: Man, 20, charged over alleged rape of elderly woman in own NT home

children.content: The man was arrested two days after allegedly fleeing the scene in Woodroffe, outside Darwin.

items.title: Meet the groundskeeper breathing life into a regional war cemetery

children.content: Tending to the living to honour the dead — that's what the dedicated groundskeeper of the Adelaide River War Cemetery says has been the pinnacle of his gardening career.

gallery.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

thumbnail.altText: A white man with thick, long rugged blonde beard, wearing brown wide-brim hait, khaki garden shirt and shorts.

items.title: Rate hikes and tax changes have broken the back of the latest property boom

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

image.altText: An overhead shot of a housing estate.

items.title: 'Intolerable': When $768m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: There are fresh concerns over the structural integrity of Hobart's new Bridgewater Bridge, which one expert who worked on the project suggested could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

image.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

items.title: Squatters take over empty spaces in Sydney social housing complex

children.content: Social housing tenants in inner Sydney have repeatedly complained about illegal squatters, they have been removed by police but they keep coming back.

image.altText: broken seats, tables inside a storrage space in a social housing complex in redfern

items.title: 'My time to go': The words that showed Daniher's humanity in tragedy

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

gallery.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: Bringing them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

image.altText: A man, wearing a tshirt and an akubra hat, stands outside in front of trees.

items.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

items.title: Richard Gadd 'couldn't escape' the story he wanted to tell after Baby Reindeer

children.content: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

items.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home

children.content: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.

items.title: What we know about the remaining families returning from Syria

children.content: The last cohort of ISIS-linked Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp are expected to return back into Australia in the coming hours. Here's what we know about them.

items.title: Australian family demands action one year after son's Bali death

children.content: A year after their 23-year-old son died in Bali in "unclear" circumstances, a Queensland couple have unanswered questions.

items.title: 'Fool, ignoramus': Parliamentary committee suspended as senators clash

items.title: Community 'devastated' by plans for gas-powered data centres

items.title: Labor calls for spare change as One Nation surges

children.content: Federal Labor is asking for donations to fight One Nation as the latest polling shows the minor party continuing to surge in key electorates, including in Western Sydney.

items.title: Push to find resting places for remains found in desecrated graves

children.content: Nine piles of dirt containing up to 5,000 human remains from dug-up graves have been dumped at the rear of a regional Victorian cemetery. A team of archaeologists is helping to find a home for the disturbed remains.

items.title: Kokkinakis wins French Open five-set thriller on mixed day for Aussies

items.title: Thai cave rescuers work to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave

children.content: Seven villagers entered the cave in central Laos five days ago looking for gold when heavy rain caused flash flooding, trapping them inside.

items.title: Factory workers reunite 16 years after closure of major food manufacturer

children.content: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

items.title: Crews to work overnight after burst pipe in Melbourne leaves thousands without power

children.content: A power company was forced to switch off power to about 10,000 customers in the heart of Melbourne after a burst water main threatened to flood a substation on Monday afternoon.

image.altText: Trucks pumping out thousands of litres of water from a flooded substation in Melbourne's CBD.
